entries.what: selectStage(id): hideToast; sets selectedPhase; history.replaceState '#s'+id; canvas stages 3/4 show #view-canvas (display:block) else #view-board; if leaving canvas→flushCanvasSave; renderBoard always; enterCanvas if canvas; then kicks the right pollers per stage. Stepper pills (renderBoard) onclick=selectStage; selected pill scrollIntoView (narrow-screen). hashchange listener also routes. poll() first-load reads #sN from hash to restore stage.
entries.portingNotes: Use a router/state for selectedPhase synced to location.hash (#sN) via replaceState + hashchange. The board↔canvas swap is display toggle of two containers — in React, conditionally render. Per-stage poller wiring (1094-1097) determines which data fetches run — model as effects keyed on selectedPhase. flushCanvasSave on canvas→board transition is essential (see canvas persistence entry).

entries.name: Polling architecture (state/inbox/health/pages/choices/update + per-stage brief/explore)
entries.what: On project load: poll() then setInterval poll 2s, pollUpdate 60s, pollInbox 4s, pollHealth 15s, pollPages 5s, pollChoices 3s, and a 2.5s per-stage tick dispatching pollBrief/pollResearch (s1), pollExplore/pollSearchLog (s2), pollExplore+pollStageRun (canvas), pollStageRun (s5-7). Each poll fetches its endpoint and re-renders the relevant section, with guards to NOT re-render while a textarea/input in that section is focused (renderStageExtra 1270-1271; pollBrief 1235; pollResearch 1401; pollStageRun 1550) or while a Viewer is open (1272).
entries.portingNotes: Many independent intervals. In React, model as useEffect timers; the focus-guard pattern (skip re-render while user typing in this section) is the recurring gotcha — controlled inputs that re-render on poll would steal focus/caret. Solution: keep editable fields as local component state decoupled from poll data, or keep poll data in a ref and only commit to render-state when the field is unfocused. Endpoints: /api/state, /api/inbox, /api/health, /api/pages, /api/choices, /api/update-check, /api/brief, /api/explore, /api/agent-log?phase=, /api/wizard, /api/deploy-creds.

entries.name: Stage-1 brief flow (description, gen-summary, clarify questions, confirm)
entries.where: console.html:1215-1403 (pBrief, briefSectionHtml, foldClarifications, genSummary, confirmBrief, doResearch)
entries.what: Textarea oninput→pBrief.description. genSummary folds clarifications (structured accumulation of picked Q/A + free text into the description with 问题澄清N/用户澄清N tags), POST /api/brief {description,request:{kind:'gen-summary'}}. AI returns summary + clarify questions (chips, single-select toggle via stage-extra delegation 1657-1660). confirmBrief finalizes without re-running AI. doResearch POST /api/research. Generation/failure state derived from server request slot presence (1229-1234).
entries.portingNotes: foldClarifications mutates and re-serializes the description (visible/editable accumulation) — port faithfully; it reads live textarea values before folding. The generating/failed state machine is inferred from whether server still has the gen-summary request slot (slot vanished + not ready = failed). Question chips are single-select-toggle stored in pBrief.answers by index. Focus-guard: don't re-render while #st-brief focused.

entries.name: Stage-2 refs panel (style tags, search, collect link, more-like, remove, select)
entries.what: Style tag chips (STAGE_TAGS) multi-select → immediate POST /api/explore {stylePrefs}. searchRefs/moreLikeRef/collectRef POST /api/explore with request kinds search-refs/collect-ref (seedRef for more-like). Ref card click toggles selectedRefs → POST /api/explore {selectedRefs}. removeRef optimistic + POST {removeRef}. Buttons: 🔍放大 (Viewer), 🔗源 (open source), ＋类似 (more-like). Request slots are per-kind (pExplore.request keyed by kind).
entries.portingNotes: All selections persist immediately to /api/explore (optimistic local + POST). Per-kind request object replaced the old single slot (compat shim at 1248). Event delegation on #stage-extra distinguishes .wz-tag (with data-qi → brief question vs data-tag → style) and .wz-ref. stopPropagation on inner buttons (放大/源/类似/删除) so card-select click doesn't fire.

entries.name: Stage-3 hero generation + stage-4 pages (genHeroes/genAllPages/runPageMap/tidyPages/addPagePrompt)
entries.what: stageCards() builds cards from pExplore.heroes (s3) or cv.pages (s4 × platform matrix). cvAutoPlaceCards grid-places new cards (GRID_COLS=4, ROW_GAP=500). genHeroes POST /api/explore gen-heroes. runPageMap/genAllPages POST /api/run-stage phase4 with long instruction strings. tidyPages re-lays cards by group/name into grid, prunes orphan item coords, cvSave+renderCanvas+cvFit. addPagePrompt uses window.prompt ×2 (name, group) → POST /api/pages {action:'add'}. pollPages 5s.
entries.portingNotes: cvAutoPlaceCards auto-assigns positions to cards lacking cv.items entries (and saves) — port the placement math. tidyPages prunes orphan 'page:' keys (deleted pages) before re-grid — keep to avoid cvFit blowup. addPagePrompt uses native prompt() — replace with a small form/modal but keep name+group fields. genAllPages has a window.confirm gate. The instruction strings are passed verbatim to the agent — preserve them (they encode the agent workflow, incl. concurrency directives).

entries.name: Stage 5/6/7 run-stage + deploy creds + ⑥ preview action
entries.what: runStageHtml builds per-phase card; main button doRunStage POST /api/run-stage {phase,instruction} (409=already running). ⑥ extra preview button doRunAction POST /api/run-action {phase,action:'preview'}. Button labels adapt to pfWizard.primary (APP/PC/H5) loaded from /api/wizard. revealCode POST /api/reveal (opens Finder/Explorer). Deploy creds (s7): inputs→pdcDraft, saveDeployCreds parses extra KEY=VALUE lines + POST /api/deploy-creds {creds}; removeCred/clearCreds. pollStageRun reads /api/agent-log?phase=stage-N for running/waiting/failed (server-authoritative running flag).
entries.portingNotes: Running/waiting/failed state comes from server (data.running/data.waiting) not log-guessing. 409 handling on double-trigger. Platform-adaptive button text depends on async-loaded wizard.primary — re-render when it arrives. Deploy-creds 'extra' textarea parses KEY=VALUE per line (regex). clearCreds uses window.confirm. doRunAction reuses phase-6 progress channel without redoing the stage.

entries.name: Home project wall + version/update banner
entries.what: pollProjects GET /api/projects (3s), renders active + archived (toggle) + pending ghost cards. projCard: cover/initial, phase segs, working badge, health dot, broken state, relTime; onclick→/p/<id>/; del button stopPropagation→openDelete. loadVersion GET /api/version (sidebar). checkUpdate GET /api/update-check?refresh=1 (confirm→doUpdate). pollUpdate 60s shows #update-bar. doUpdate POST /api/update.
entries.portingNotes: Straightforward list rendering + intervals. onclick inline navigation and stopPropagation on delete button → in React use onClick with e.stopPropagation. Update flow uses window.confirm/alert. Version text in sidebar is clickable (checkUpdate).

entries.name: Global keyboard: Escape closes modals/preview
entries.what: document keydown: Escape → closeModal() + closePreview() (closes both the artifact modal and the圈选 overlay).
entries.portingNotes: Single global Escape handler closing whichever overlay is open. In React, a top-level useEffect keydown listener, or per-modal onKeyDown. Note Viewer.js handles its OWN Escape internally (keyboard:true) — don't double-bind in a way that fights it.

interactions.notes:
File: /Users/hong/.claude/skills/productflow/assets/console.html (2538 lines; CSS 7-544, body 547-704, JS 705-2536). Single-file vanilla app, no framework — all rendering via innerHTML string templates + esc() escaping; all interactivity via inline onclick/oninput/onsubmit attributes PLUS several event-delegation IIFEs (#new-body, #choices-bar, #stage-extra, #pv-stage, #pv-boxes, #view-canvas).

IMPERATIVE LIBS (3): (1) Viewer.js (/vendor/viewer.min.js + .css, loaded eagerly) for ref image zoom/pan/pager — operates on a hidden DOM gallery, must destroy()+recreate, and re-renders are GUARDED while it's open (refsViewerOpen). (2) markmap (d3.min.js + markmap-lib.js + markmap-view.js, lazy-loaded in order via injected <script>, mmLoaded flag) renders .md mindmaps into an <svg id=mm-svg> imperatively. (3) No other libs — icons are inline Lucide SVG strings (IC_*, deviceIcon, docIco).

COORDINATE SYSTEMS (the porting minefield): (a) Canvas world transform = translate(view.x,view.y) scale(view.z) on #cv-world, origin 0 0. Screen→world: drag delta /view.z; zoom-anchor keeps cursor point fixed via x=cx-(cx-x)*k. Card positions stored in WORLD px in cv.items{id:{x,y}}. (b) Annotation boxes stored NORMALIZED 0..1 (frac() = (clientX-rect.left)/rect.width clamped), toFixed(4) on wire — display-size independent so the agent can remap to source pixels. Two totally separate coordinate models — don't conflate.

POINTER EVENTS: both the canvas controller and the annotation drawer use raw Pointer Events + setPointerCapture + a move threshold (3px canvas / 0.02 normalized annotation). Canvas pointerdown bails via e.target.closest() on an allowlist of interactive subzones — this dispatch must be replicated or drags hijack buttons. wheel needs {passive:false}+preventDefault (attach via ref/addEventListener, NOT JSX onWheel) or browser zoom/scroll leaks; ctrl/meta+wheel routes trackpad-pinch to zoom.

RECURRING REACT GOTCHA — focus/caret preservation: the app polls every 2-2.5s and re-renders sections via innerHTML, but EVERY editable section guards against re-render while its textarea/input is focused (renderStageExtra, pollBrief, pollResearch, pollStageRun, renderChoices, openInbox sticky-scroll, refsViewerOpen). A naive controlled-input React port that re-renders on each poll WILL steal focus/caret and break typing, drag, and the open Viewer. Strategy: decouple editable/in-progress UI state from poll-driven data (refs or local state), commit only when unfocused/idle; keep stable keys so React preserves focused nodes.

localStorage: only the new-project draft (key 'pf-new-project-draft', saved on every keystroke/toggle, cleared on successful create). Canvas positions are NOT localStorage — they persist server-side via /api/canvas (per stage, 600ms debounce + flush-on-leave capturing the leaving stage id). NOTE: the cv.notes array and a 'cv positions' localStorage are referenced conceptually but in THIS file notes are server-persisted alongside items and there is no localStorage for canvas — only NM draft uses localStorage.

KEYBOARD: chat Enter=send / Shift+Enter=newline (form.requestSubmit); global Escape closes modal+preview; Viewer.js has its own keyboard:true (arrows/esc) — don't fight it.

ENDPOINTS touched: /api/projects, /api/create, /api/project-remove, /api/project-delete, /api/state, /api/inbox (GET+POST, types: plain, preview-feedback; legacy canvas-feedback render), /api/health, /api/choices, /api/choice, /api/brief (GET+POST), /api/research, /api/explore (GET+POST: stylePrefs/selectedRefs/selectedHero/removeRef/removeHero/request{search-refs,collect-ref,gen-heroes}), /api/agent-log?phase=, /api/canvas (GET ?stage / POST), /api/pages (GET+POST: add/remove/gen-version), /api/redraw, /api/run-stage (409 guard), /api/run-action, /api/reveal, /api/wizard, /api/deploy-creds (GET+POST: creds/remove/clear), /api/version, /api/update-check (?refresh=1), /api/update. Project-scoped calls prefix PF_BASE=/p/<id>; global calls (projects/version/update/create) are unprefixed. Routing: location.pathname /p/<id>/ → project view, else home; stage in location.hash #sN.

Legacy/dead-ish code to be aware of (do NOT silently drop without asking): inbox renders canvas-feedback type (❤likes/comments) though the current canvas emits no likes; PLAT/wizard fields. Compat banner warns on non-7-phase projects.

entries.name: Routing model: global vs per-project (/p/<pid>/...)
entries.what: Two route namespaces. GLOBAL routes (no prefix): /, /api/version, /api/update-check, /api/projects, /vendor/<name>, plus POST /api/update, /api/create, /api/project-remove, /api/project-delete, /api/pending. PER-PROJECT routes match P_ROUTE = ^/p/([a-z0-9-]{1,64})(/.*)? — the 'sub' tail after the pid is the actual api path (/api/state, /api/inbox, etc.). So the prompt's '/api/state' etc. live at /p/<pid>/api/state. pid resolved to abs project path via _resolve() reading ~/.productflow/projects/<pid>.json; pf = <path>/.productflow.
entries.portingNotes: WS layer must key every channel by (pid, channel). React router/WS client should mirror this: a global channel (projects/version) + per-project channels. ID validation regex ID_RE=^[a-z0-9-]{1,64}$ must be preserved server-side. Note do_POST whitelists allowed sub paths at line 1436 (404 otherwise) — keep that allowlist when adding WS endpoints.

entries.name: GET / and GET /p/<pid>/ and /p/<pid> (console HTML)
entries.what: Serves the single-page console: reads assets/console.html raw, sends text/html, Cache-Control no-store. Same HTML for global dashboard and per-project view (front-end reads pid from URL). 500 'console.html missing' if file absent.
entries.portingNotes: This is THE repointing target for a built React dist/. Change _console() to serve dist/index.html, and add a catch-all that serves hashed JS/CSS assets from dist/ (currently only /vendor/ whitelist exists; built bundles need their own static handler with correct content-types + long-cache for hashed files, but index.html stays no-store). SPA fallback: /p/<pid>/ already returns index HTML — keep that so client routing works.

entries.name: GET /vendor/<name> (whitelisted vendor JS/CSS)
entries.what: Serves a fixed whitelist of 5 files from SKILL_DIR/assets/vendor/: d3.min.js, markmap-lib.js, markmap-view.js, viewer.min.js, viewer.min.css (content-type from a dict). Anything not in the dict -> 404. 404 if file missing on disk.
entries.portingNotes: If React bundles these via npm (d3, markmap, viewerjs), the /vendor route can be dropped — but verify console.html's usages first. If kept, it must coexist with the new dist/ static handler. The hardcoded content-type dict pattern is the template to reuse for serving dist assets.

entries.name: GET /api/version
entries.what: Returns {app:'productflow', version:VERSION}. Also used by _probe_version() (line 1796) to detect an already-running instance on the port.
entries.portingNotes: No backing project file — VERSION read once at module load from skill-root VERSION file. Keep stable; the singleton-port detection depends on the exact {app:'productflow'} shape.

entries.name: GET /api/update-check?refresh=1
entries.what: Compares local VERSION to remote GitHub VERSION (raw.githubusercontent.com/.../productflow/VERSION). Returns {current, latest, update_available(bool), repo:'hongnono-wdh/productflow', git:bool(is-git-checkout)}. ?refresh=1 re-fetches synchronously (network, ~4s timeout); otherwise uses cached _latest_version (refreshed once in bg at startup, line 1854).
entries.portingNotes: Network call — keep the cached-vs-refresh split so the default poll is cheap. WS could push 'update_available' once instead of front-end polling. _latest_version is module-global mutated by bg thread and by POST /api/update.

entries.name: GET /api/projects (global dashboard payload)
entries.what: Returns {version, projects:[...], pending:[...]}. Each project summary: {id,name,dir_label,phases:[{id,status}],done,current_phase,updated,cover(/p/<pid>/artifacts/...),working(updated<120s ago),missing,error,archived,health}. Sources: registry ~/.productflow/projects/<pid>.json + each project's .productflow/state.json + .productflow/health.json. pending from ~/.productflow/pending/*.json -> {file,name,brief,created}.
entries.portingNotes: Heaviest read — iterates ALL projects, opening state.json+health.json per project on every poll. For WS push, watch mtime of: each projects/<pid>.json (registry), each <proj>/.productflow/state.json, each <proj>/.productflow/health.json, and pending/ dir. 'working' is time-derived (updated within 120s) so it changes WITHOUT a file write — WS can't rely purely on mtime for the working flag; recompute on each push or tick. cover URL is /p/<pid>/artifacts/<rel> (rel stripped of leading 'artifacts/').

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/state
entries.what: Streams raw .productflow/state.json bytes (application/json). 404 {error:'not_initialized'} if missing. state.json holds: id, product, phases[{id,status,artifacts[{type,file,ts,title}]}], current_phase, updated.
entries.portingNotes: PRIMARY per-project channel. WS: watch mtime of <proj>/.productflow/state.json. Note it streams the file verbatim (no re-serialization) — preserve that to avoid altering agent-written content. state.json is written by pf_state.py (CLI used by spawned agents), NOT by this server, so mtime watching is the only change-detection signal.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/inbox
entries.what: Reads .productflow/inbox.jsonl line-by-line, returns {messages:[obj,...]} (each line a JSON msg). Missing -> {messages:[]}. Messages appended by both this server (web-origin: explore-request, brief-request, research-request, stage-request, action-request, choice-answer, page-version-request) and by agents.
entries.portingNotes: WS: watch mtime of inbox.jsonl; it's append-only so a tail/incremental-push is natural (send only new lines past last offset). Many POST handlers append here — those writes should trigger the inbox channel push.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/agent-log?phase=<phase>
entries.what: Per-phase progress stream. phase param (default 'brief') maps to .productflow/agent-log-<safe>.jsonl (safe = lowercased, non [a-z0-9-] -> '-'). Returns {lines:last-50 parsed JSON lines, running, waiting}. For phase matching ^stage-(\d+)$: running = (pid,N) in _STAGE_RUNNING (in-memory set), waiting = running AND _has_open_choice(pf,N) (reads choices.json for unanswered choice of that stage). Known phases: brief, research, search-refs, gen-heroes, collect-ref, stage-3, stage-4, stage-5, stage-6, stage-7.
entries.portingNotes: MOST polling-heavy channel — front-end currently polls this fast for live agent progress; prime WS-push candidate. running/waiting come from IN-MEMORY _STAGE_RUNNING (not a file) — WS layer must push status changes when the set mutates (add at run-stage/run-action POST, discard in _auto_stage/_auto_action finally blocks lines 507-508, 548-549) AND when choices.json changes (waiting flips). agent-log files are append-only -> incremental push works. Channel key is (pid, phase). Many distinct log files per project.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/canvas?stage=N
entries.what: Returns the per-stage cell of .productflow/canvas.json. canvas.json = {"3":{view,items,notes}, "4":{...}}. ?stage=N returns data[N]; missing/non-dict -> {view:null,items:{},notes:[]}.
entries.portingNotes: WS: watch canvas.json mtime; push only the changed stage cell. Note read path re-parses and slices by stage; write path (POST) is _CANVAS_LOCK-serialized read-modify-write across stages — concurrent P3/P4 save timers. WS push must send per-stage so two canvases don't clobber each other's view state on the client.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/health
entries.what: Streams .productflow/health.json raw. Missing -> {}. Content {url,ok,status,ms,checked} written by the background health loop (only for projects with deploy.json containing a url).
entries.portingNotes: Written ONLY by server's _health_loop (every 300s, line 1793), so mtime changes at most every 5 min — cheap WS channel. Also surfaced inside /api/projects summary, so a health write should push BOTH the per-project health channel and the global projects channel.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/pages
entries.what: Streams .productflow/pages.json raw. Missing -> {pages:[]}. pages.json = {pages:[{id,name,group,status,versions[],note}]}. Same file mutated by pf_state 'page' CLI and by POST /api/pages.
entries.portingNotes: WS: watch pages.json mtime. Both server (POST add/remove/gen-version) and agents (pf_state page set) write it — mtime watch covers both.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/choices
entries.what: Streams .productflow/choices.json raw. Missing -> {choices:[]}. choices.json = {choices:[{id(ch-xxxx),stage,question,options[],answer}]}. Created by pf_state 'choice ask' (agent); answered by POST /api/choice.
entries.portingNotes: WS: watch choices.json mtime. This drives the 'agent is waiting for your answer' UI; also feeds the 'waiting' flag in agent-log. Push must coordinate with agent-log channel (waiting recomputation).

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/deploy-creds
entries.what: Returns {keys:[{key,masked}]} — lists stored deploy-cred KEYS with masked values (only last 4 chars). Reads ~/.productflow/secrets/<pid>.env (export K=V), NOT the project's .productflow dir (secrets live outside the product git repo to avoid leakage). NEVER returns plaintext.
entries.portingNotes: Backing file is OUTSIDE .productflow: ~/.productflow/secrets/<pid>.env (mode 600, dir 700). WS: watch that file's mtime if live-updating the creds panel (low value — rarely changes). Critical: keep masking; never push plaintext over WS.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/explore
entries.what: Streams .productflow/explore.json raw. Missing -> {stylePrefs[],request{},refs[],selectedRefs[],styleSummary,heroes[],selectedHero}. Holds phase-2/3 visual exploration state: refs[{id,file,title,source}], heroes[{id,file,style}], selectedRefs[ids], selectedHero(file), request{search-refs|gen-heroes|collect-ref:{...}}, heroGenFailed flag, gen-records.
entries.portingNotes: WS: watch explore.json mtime. Heavily mutated: by POST /api/explore (web) AND by agents via pf_state explore add-ref/add-hero/done-request/set-summary/gen-record AND by server cleanup (_clear_explore_slot, _sweep_stale_explore_slots). The 'request' sub-slots drive the 'generating...' spinner — front-end polls this during gen. Prime WS channel for phases 2/3.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/brief
entries.what: Streams .productflow/brief.json raw. Missing -> {description,request:null,questions[],confirmed:false,summary:{goal,users,need,scope},ready:false}. brief.json holds the AI product-understanding summary + clarifying questions[{q,options[]}] + confirmed/ready flags + request slot (gen-summary).
entries.portingNotes: WS: watch brief.json mtime. Mutated by POST /api/brief (web) and by background _auto_gen_brief (server writes summary/questions/ready, line 257). The request slot drives a 'generating summary' spinner — front-end polls during gen. _clear_brief_request clears stuck slots.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/api/wizard
entries.what: Streams .productflow/wizard.json raw. Missing -> {brief,platforms[],primary:null,priority[],stylePrefs[]}. Holds wizard-collected config: brief, platforms (PC/H5/APP), primary platform, priority. Written at /api/create (line 1350) from wizard data.
entries.portingNotes: WS: low priority — written once at create, rarely after. primary is read server-side by many agent spawners (_read_primary) to pick image sizes. Largely static per project.

entries.name: GET /p/<pid>/artifacts/<rel> (static artifact files)
entries.what: Serves files under <proj>/.productflow/artifacts/. Path normalized + realpath-anchored to artifacts base (403 on traversal). Content-type by ext: png/jpg/jpeg/webp/svg/html/json, else text/plain. 404 on missing/dir.
entries.portingNotes: This is how all generated images/HTML/analysis reach the UI (and project covers). Must remain reachable from React (img src=/p/<pid>/artifacts/...). When repointing static serving to dist/, do NOT route /p/<pid>/artifacts/ through dist — it's project data, separate base. New images appear as agents write files; WS could push 'new artifact' via state.json/explore.json mtime rather than watching the artifacts dir directly.

entries.name: POST /api/update (global)
entries.what: Self-update: git -C <repo> pull --ff-only, then runs scripts/migrate.py if present. Returns {ok,version,restart_needed,git,migrate} or errors {not_git_checkout|git_pull_failed}. Mutates module-global _latest_version.
entries.portingNotes: Server-side git/subprocess; not a data channel. After update, restart_needed signals the running server is stale (VERSION read at module load). WS could push a 'restart needed' banner. No project backing file.

entries.name: POST /api/create (global)
entries.what: Creates a real project under ~/code/<slug> (collision -> -N suffix), calls pf_state.create_project, writes wizard.json from {brief,platforms,primary,priority}. Request {name,slug?,brief?,platforms?,primary?,priority?}. Returns {ok,id,dir}.
entries.portingNotes: Creates a new project dir + registry entry + state.json + wizard.json -> must trigger global /api/projects push. New per-project channels become available afterward. Slug sanitization (re.sub [^a-z0-9-]) + base ~/code must be preserved.

entries.name: POST /api/project-remove (global)
entries.what: Request {id}. Removes ONLY the registry entry ~/.productflow/projects/<id>.json; disk project files untouched. Returns {ok}. Bad id -> 400.
entries.portingNotes: Mutates registry -> push global /api/projects. Disk files remain (re-discoverable). Distinguish from project-delete in UI/WS events.

entries.name: POST /api/project-delete (global)
entries.what: Request {id}. Hard delete: validates target is under $HOME, not $HOME itself, contains .productflow (unsafe_path 400 otherwise), shutil.rmtree the project dir, removes registry entry AND secrets/<id>.env. Returns {ok}.
entries.portingNotes: Destructive — preserve ALL safety checks verbatim. Triggers global /api/projects push and tears down per-project channels (WS must close/notify subscribers to that pid). Also deletes secrets file.

entries.name: POST /api/pending (global)
entries.what: Request {name,brief?,platforms?,primary?,priority?}. Writes ~/.productflow/pending/<UTCstamp>-<hex>.json = {name,brief,created,v:1,platforms?,primary?,priority?}. Returns {ok,file}. empty_name -> 400.
entries.portingNotes: A pending project (queued for an agent to pick up). Triggers global /api/projects push (pending[] section). Watch pending/ dir mtime for WS.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/inbox (generic message)
entries.where: do_POST 1740-1748 (fall-through after all specific sub handlers)
entries.what: Generic web->agent message. Request {text, ...extra}. Appends {ts,from:'web',text,...extra} to inbox.jsonl. Returns {ok}. empty text -> 400. This is the default handler for /api/inbox sub (and any sub that falls through the specific if-chain).
entries.portingNotes: Appends to inbox.jsonl -> push inbox channel. Note it spreads arbitrary extra keys from the request body into the message. WS client send-message maps here.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/canvas
entries.what: Request {stage:'3'|'4',view,items,notes}. Under _CANVAS_LOCK, read-modify-write canvas.json updating ONLY that stage cell (preserves other stages). Returns {ok}. bad_stage 400 for non 3/4.
entries.portingNotes: Two front-end canvases (P3/P4) auto-save concurrently -> lock is essential; keep it. After write, push canvas channel for that stage only. WS echo of own writes could cause canvas state churn — front-end may need to ignore self-originated pushes.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/pages
entries.what: Request {action:'add'|'remove'|'gen-version', ...}. add: {name,group?} -> appends page {id:pg-<hex>,name,group,status:placeholder,versions[],note}. remove: {id}. gen-version: {id,platform:PC|H5|APP} -> appends inbox msg + spawns _auto_page_version thread (returns early, agent writes versions via pf_state). Writes pages.json (except gen-version which returns before re-write). Returns {ok} / 400 errors.
entries.portingNotes: gen-version spawns a background agent (claude subprocess) and appends to inbox -> push BOTH pages and inbox channels; progress shows in agent-log phase 'stage-4'. add/remove mutate pages.json -> push pages channel. Distinguish the three actions in WS event semantics.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/explore
entries.what: Request can set {stylePrefs,selectedRefs,selectedHero}, {removeRef:id} / {removeHero:id} (also deletes disk file via pf_state._rm_artifact_file), and {request:{kind:'search-refs'|'gen-heroes'|'collect-ref',...}}. A request is slotted into explore.json.request[kind], appends inbox 'explore-request', and spawns _auto_explore thread (claude agent doing playwright search / openai image gen). Writes explore.json. Returns {ok}.
entries.portingNotes: The request kinds kick off long-running bg agents whose progress streams to agent-log phases search-refs/gen-heroes/collect-ref and whose results land in explore.json (refs/heroes) + artifacts/. WS: push explore + inbox channels on write; agent progress via the agent-log channels; gen results via explore + state mtime. Stuck slots auto-cleared at thread end (line 927) -> another explore push.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/brief
entries.what: Request can set {description, questions[], confirmed(bool)} and {request:{kind:'gen-summary',description?}}. A gen-summary request sets ready=false, clears old questions, appends inbox 'brief-request', spawns _auto_gen_brief thread (claude summarizes -> writes summary/questions/ready/confirmed=false). Writes brief.json. Returns {ok}.
entries.portingNotes: gen-summary spawns bg agent; progress in agent-log phase 'brief'; result written to brief.json by server thread. WS: push brief + inbox on write; brief channel again when _auto_gen_brief finishes (or clears stuck request). 'ready' flag transition is the key UI signal.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/research
entries.what: Request {instruction?}. Appends inbox 'research-request' and spawns _auto_research thread (claude runs full phase-1 market research: competitor search, screenshots, analysis; registers artifacts/steps via pf_state). Returns {ok}.
entries.portingNotes: No dedicated backing file beyond inbox; progress streams to agent-log phase 'research'; outputs land in state.json (phases/artifacts) + artifacts/phase-1/. WS: push inbox on accept; live progress via agent-log 'research' channel; results via state channel. NOTE: no _STAGE_RUNNING guard for research (unlike run-stage) — can be double-triggered.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/choice
entries.what: Request {id,answer}. Sets answer on the matching choice in choices.json, appends inbox 'choice-answer' if found. Returns {ok}. This unblocks an agent waiting in pf_state 'choice wait'.
entries.portingNotes: Writing the answer flips the agent-log 'waiting' flag (via _has_open_choice) and unblocks the agent (which polls choices.json). WS: push choices + inbox channels, and recompute/push the relevant agent-log stage 'waiting' status. Tight coupling between choices and agent-log channels.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/run-stage
entries.what: Request {phase:int(4-7),instruction?}. Concurrency guard: if (pid,phase) in _STAGE_RUNNING -> 409 already_running; else add to set, append inbox 'stage-request', spawn _auto_stage thread (claude runs phase N per phase-N-*.md, may inject deploy creds for phase 7). Returns {ok} or 409/400(bad_phase).
entries.portingNotes: _STAGE_RUNNING is the in-memory source of truth for running/waiting in agent-log. WS must push agent-log stage-N status when the set is added here and discarded in _auto_stage finally (507-508). 409 already_running must surface in UI. Progress channel = agent-log phase 'stage-N'; results = state.json + artifacts.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/run-action
entries.what: Request {phase:6,action:'preview'} (only this combo valid; else bad_action 400). Shares (pid,6) _STAGE_RUNNING guard with run-stage (409 already_running). Appends inbox 'action-request', spawns _auto_action thread (build+launch product for live preview, does NOT mark phase done). Returns {ok}.
entries.portingNotes: Shares phase-6 concurrency guard and the 'stage-6' agent-log channel with run-stage — WS status for stage-6 covers both. Currently only preview; treat action as an enum for future. discard happens in _auto_action finally (548-549).

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/deploy-creds
entries.what: Manages ~/.productflow/secrets/<pid>.env. Modes: {clear:true} wipe all; {remove:KEY} drop one; {creds:{...},replace?:true} merge-or-replace. Keys must match CRED_KEY_RE. Returns {ok,count}. bad_creds 400 if creds not dict.
entries.portingNotes: Writes secrets OUTSIDE .productflow (mode 600). After write, push the deploy-creds channel (masked only!). Values shell-escaped via shlex.quote. Never echo plaintext over WS. count is the number of stored keys.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/reveal
entries.what: Request {path?}. Opens the project dir (or a traversal-checked subpath) in the OS file manager (open/explorer/xdg-open). Returns {ok,path} or {ok:false,error} 500. Local-only convenience.
entries.portingNotes: Side-effect only (spawns OS file manager), no data/WS channel. Pure RPC; React keeps it as a button -> POST. Path-traversal anchoring to project root must stay.

entries.name: POST /p/<pid>/api/redraw
entries.what: Request {stage:3|4,file(under artifacts/),regions:list,prompt(required),platform?,pageId?}. Validates file is real & under artifacts/. Spawns _auto_redraw thread: builds inpaint mask from regions (empty regions = whole-image edit), calls edit.py (gpt-image-2) -> new image saved as a new version (stage3: explore add-hero; pageId: page add-version; else artifact 4). Returns {ok}. Errors: bad_redraw_req/bad_file 400.
entries.portingNotes: Long-running bg image edit; progress streams to agent-log phase 'stage-3' or 'stage-4'; result lands as new version in explore.json (hero) or pages.json (page version) or state artifacts. WS: push the matching agent-log stage channel for progress, then explore/pages/state channel when done. Requires Pillow + edit.py (PF_EDIT_PY override).

entries.name: Background loops & threading model
entries.what: ThreadingHTTPServer = one OS thread per request. Long-running work (claude/image agents) runs in detached daemon Threads, writing files that GET routes read. _health_loop sweeps all projects every 300s, writing health.json (only for projects with deploy.json+url). At startup: _sweep_stale_explore_slots clears orphan explore/brief request slots from a dead prior process; _refresh_latest pulls remote version once in bg. Module globals: _latest_version, _STAGE_RUNNING (set, guarded by _STAGE_RUN_LOCK), _CANVAS_LOCK.
entries.portingNotes: For WS push, two change sources: (a) FILE mtime changes (state/inbox/explore/brief/canvas/pages/choices/health.json + artifacts) written by both this server's bg threads and external pf_state CLI -> a single mtime-watcher (per-project) covers all; (b) IN-MEMORY state changes (_STAGE_RUNNING, _latest_version) that have NO file -> WS must push explicitly at mutation points. The 'working' flag in /api/projects is TIME-derived (no file event) -> needs a periodic tick. Threading model already supports a long-lived WS connection thread alongside request threads, but ThreadingHTTPServer's BaseHTTPRequestHandler does NOT speak the WS upgrade handshake — you'll need to implement Upgrade handling in do_GET or swap to an asgi/websocket-capable server. Health cadence is 300s; remote-version cadence is once-at-startup (+ on-demand refresh).

endpoints.notes:
Key cross-cutting facts for the WS+React rewrite:

1) ROUTE SHAPE CORRECTION: the prompt lists '/api/state', '/api/inbox', '/api/canvas', etc. as if top-level, but they are ALL per-project under /p/<pid>/api/... (the 'sub' tail after P_ROUTE matches the pid). Only /, /vendor/, /api/version, /api/update-check, /api/projects, /api/update, /api/create, /api/project-remove, /api/project-delete, /api/pending are truly global. There is NO '/api/deploy-creds' top-level — it's /p/<pid>/api/deploy-creds. (Verify against console.html fetch() calls when porting.)

2) BACKING FILES (all under <project>/.productflow/ unless noted):
   - state.json  -> /api/state, /api/projects (per-proj summary)
   - inbox.jsonl -> /api/inbox (append-only)
   - agent-log-<phase>.jsonl -> /api/agent-log (append-only, MANY files: brief, research, search-refs, gen-heroes, collect-ref, stage-3..7)
   - canvas.json -> /api/canvas (namespaced by stage '3'/'4')
   - health.json -> /api/health + /api/projects (written ONLY by server health loop, 300s)
   - pages.json  -> /api/pages
   - choices.json-> /api/choices (also feeds agent-log 'waiting')
   - explore.json-> /api/explore (refs/heroes/request slots)
   - brief.json  -> /api/brief
   - wizard.json -> /api/wizard (written at /api/create; read by many agent spawners via _read_primary)
   - deploy.json -> NOT served via API; consumed by _health_sweep to know the URL to ping
   - artifacts/  -> /p/<pid>/artifacts/<rel> (images/html/json), traversal-guarded
   OUTSIDE .productflow:
   - ~/.productflow/projects/<pid>.json  = registry (id->path,archived,created)
   - ~/.productflow/pending/*.json       = queued projects
   - ~/.productflow/secrets/<pid>.env    = deploy creds (mode 600), masked-only over API
   - SKILL_DIR/VERSION                   = version source
   - SKILL_DIR/assets/console.html       = the SPA  (REPOINT TARGET -> dist/index.html)
   - SKILL_DIR/assets/vendor/<5 files>   = whitelisted vendor JS/CSS

3) WS PER-CHANNEL CHANGE DETECTION via mtime is viable for nearly every channel because the actual writers are external (pf_state.py CLI run by spawned claude agents) — the server doesn't even mediate those writes, so polling-replacement via an mtime watcher is the clean approach. Append-only files (inbox.jsonl, agent-log-*.jsonl) support incremental tail pushes. EXCEPTIONS that have NO file event: (a) _STAGE_RUNNING running/waiting flags in /api/agent-log for stage-N (in-memory set), (b) 'working' flag in /api/projects (derived from updated-within-120s clock), (c) _latest_version for /api/update-check. These need explicit push at mutation points or a periodic tick.

4) HIGHEST-FREQUENCY POLLERS (best WS payoff): /api/agent-log (live agent progress), /api/explore (gen spinner), /api/brief (summary spinner), /api/projects (dashboard). 

5) STATIC REPOINTING: _console() (line 1107) currently serves assets/console.html for both '/' and '/p/<pid>/' (SPA fallback already correct). To serve a built React dist: (a) point _console at dist/index.html (keep Cache-Control no-store on it), (b) add a static handler for dist's hashed JS/CSS/asset files with correct content-types (mirror the /vendor content-type dict pattern) and long-cache for hashed names, (c) keep /p/<pid>/artifacts/ as a SEPARATE base (project data, not bundle), (d) decide whether /vendor stays or vendor libs get bundled by npm.

6) WS HANDSHAKE GOTCHA: BaseHTTPRequestHandler/ThreadingHTTPServer has no WebSocket upgrade support. Adding WS means either implementing the Upgrade/Sec-WebSocket-Accept handshake by hand inside do_GET (then hijacking self.connection for frame I/O on its own thread), or fronting/replacing with an ASGI server (e.g. uvicorn+websockets/starlette). Given the all-stdlib, zero-dependency design of this server (only urllib/http.server), a hand-rolled WS or the smallest possible dependency is most in keeping with the project.

7) SECURITY to preserve: _host_ok (only 127.0.0.1/localhost Host), _post_origin_ok (Origin + Sec-Fetch-Site CSRF guard) — WS upgrade handler must apply the SAME checks (and validate Origin for WS). ID_RE/CRED_KEY_RE validation, artifacts/secrets path-traversal anchoring, deploy-creds masking, and the project-delete safety gates must all carry over verbatim.

8) CONCURRENCY: _CANVAS_LOCK serializes canvas.json RMW; _STAGE_RUN_LOCK guards _STAGE_RUNNING. run-stage and run-action share the (pid,6) guard and the stage-6 agent-log channel. research/redraw/explore/brief/page-version spawn unguarded bg threads (only stage 4-7 via run-stage/run-action are guarded).

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/state.json (written by pf_state.py CLI, not the server)
channels.consumers: root state store -> renderBoard (#stepper,#phase-title,#steps,#gallery,#log,#product,#meta,#next-stage-btn), renderCompatBanner, selectStage/nextStage, previewSectionHtml(P6 screenshots from phases[6].artifacts), openOverview (state artifacts), briefSectionHtml(phases[1].steps). NOTE: home card data is ALSO derived per-project from state.json -> see projects channel.

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/inbox.jsonl (append-only; written by server POST handlers AND agents)
channels.consumers: chat drawer #msgs (renderInbox), #chat-btn .has-unread dot (lastMsgCount vs chatSeen). 3 message body variants: plain, canvas-feedback(legacy ❤/comments still rendered), preview-feedback(regions). Incremental tail push viable.

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/health.json (written ONLY by server _health_loop every 300s, for projects with deploy.json url)
channels.consumers: #health-line (renderHealth). Slow channel. Same data ALSO surfaces on home cards (p.health) -> one health write should push BOTH this and the projects channel.

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/pages.json (written by server POST add/remove/gen-version AND pf_state page CLI)
channels.consumers: cv.pages -> renderCanvas stage-4 page cards (pageCardHtml2: thumbnail img + PC/H5/APP platform matrix), openOverview P4 grid. FLICKER-PRONE: key by page id + version file.

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/choices.json (written by pf_state 'choice ask'; answered by server POST /api/choice)
channels.consumers: pChoices -> #choices-bar (renderChoices floating cards, per-choice c.stage label '阶段 N'). ALSO feeds agent-log:stage-<n> 'waiting' flag via _has_open_choice -> a choices write must also recompute/push the matching agent-log waiting status. Focus-guard: skip render while a bar input is focused.

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/brief.json (written by server POST /api/brief and bg _auto_gen_brief thread)
channels.consumers: pBrief -> briefSectionHtml (P1 summary ready/running/failed states, clarify question chips, confirm). Heavy local-vs-server reconciliation (confirm-lag guard, generating/failed derived from request slot presence). Keep description/clarify/answers local.

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/explore.json (written by server POST /api/explore, bg _auto_explore thread, pf_state explore CLI, _clear_explore_slot/_sweep_stale_explore_slots)
channels.consumers: pExplore -> drives THREE views: P2 refsPanelHtml (refs grid + Viewer.js), P3 canvas hero cards + #hero-dialog (heroGenLog history), openOverview. request{} per-kind in-flight slots (search-refs/collect-ref/gen-heroes) drive spinners. FLICKER-PRONE. searchFailed comes from agent-log:search-refs, not this file.
researchLog (P1 market research tail line + running/failed). Local field researchLog.instr must survive pushes.

NOT directly consumed by current poller dispatch (P3 hero gen state is read from explore.json request slot + heroGenFailed, not these logs) — but these log files exist server-side and back /api/agent-log?phase=gen-heroes|collect-ref. Flag: the rewrite may want them for richer progress, but parity = they are not currently surfaced. Confirm before wiring.

pStageRun[n] -> runStageHtml (P5/6/7 running/waiting/failed box), stagebarHtml (P4). CRITICAL: data.running/data.waiting are SERVER RUNTIME flags (from _STAGE_RUNNING + _has_open_choice), not jsonl tail — WS push MUST carry them and fire on _STAGE_RUNNING mutation (run-stage/run-action add; _auto_stage/_auto_action finally discard) AND on choices.json change. Keep pStageRun[n].instr local.
pfWizard {primary,platforms} -> P6 preview button copy (📱模拟器/🖥本地运行/🌐本地预览) + P7 deploy button copy (分发包/部署上线). Drives button COPY only. Simplest: fetch once on mount.

homeData -> renderHome (#proj-grid projCard, #arch-grid, ghostCard pending). GOTCHA: 'working' flag is TIME-derived (state.updated within 120s) -> changes with NO file write; recompute on each push or a periodic tick. cover URL = /p/<pid>/artifacts/<rel>. FLICKER-PRONE cover img.

channels.backingFiles: SKILL_DIR/VERSION + in-memory _latest_version (refreshed once at startup; mutated by POST /api/update)
channels.consumers: #sb-ver sidebar label, #update-bar banner (loadVersion + pollUpdate). GLOBAL routes /api/version + /api/update-check (NO /p/<id> prefix). Could remain a 60s poll; not worth a per-file watcher.

channels.backingFiles: .productflow/canvas.json (per-stage keys '3'/'4'; written by THIS client, debounced 600ms, _CANVAS_LOCK serialized)
channels.consumers: cv.view/cv.items/cv.notes (USER-OWNED layout). Do NOT echo client writes back as a push (fights live drag; renderCanvas bails on cvDragging/cvEditing). GET on enterCanvas + debounced POST + flushCanvasSave on leave. Two stages never share state. Server must push per-stage cell only if ever made a channel.
pDeployCreds (P7). Mutated only by this UI's own POSTs -> optimistic update + refetch. NEVER push plaintext.

channels.channel: client->server POST actions (stay POST; result flows back via matching channel)
channels.consumers: All mutations remain POST; the resulting file change flows back via the matching WS channel -> enables optimistic-update-then-confirm. /api/reveal & /api/update are pure side-effect RPC (no channel). Concurrency 409 must surface as toast.

diffCheckpoints.compare: Old vs new screenshot at identical viewport: card grid columns (auto-fill minmax(280px,1fr) gap18), cover img vs .ph first-letter placeholder, .pname inline badges (.badge-working '进行中', .hdot health dot + ms), .segs per-phase 5px bars (done=ink/active=dim), 'N/7 阶段 · relTime', .pcard.broken opacity .6 with '目录缺失'/'状态损坏' warntag, ghost 'CLI 待接单' cards, archive section toggle. CRITICAL: confirm covers do NOT flicker/blink on the 3s refresh (keyed imgs, stable src).

232px vs 64px width; labels (.name/.lbl/.meta) hidden when collapsed; .sb-collapse visible only on hover (expanded) and hidden when collapsed; logo doubles as expand affordance (collapsed click=expand, expanded click=navigate /); nav .on state (block bg + font-weight 600); 资产/设置 stub toast '即将上线'; #sb-ver dotted underline + click=checkUpdate. Diff body.collapsed descendant selectors.

At wide AND @media<=1180px: .sel pill (inset ink ring + filled ink number + bold label) decoupled from .done (check icon IC_CHECK instead of number). At 1180px all pills collapse to number-only EXCEPT .sel keeps its label. step-sep 18px->8px. Selected pill auto-scrollIntoView(inline:center). Verify a pill that is both done+sel renders ring + check.

Summary box 4 states (ready 4 wz-airow / running spinner aibox / failed red-tinted #e0b4b4 / idle prompt); clarify question chips (single-select toggle) + #st-clarify; button variants (重新生成+✓确认 vs single 生成摘要); market-research card running/failed (#f0e2c8 amber). EDGE: type in #st-brief during a poll -> field must NOT reset (focus guard). Verify foldClarifications '问题澄清N/用户澄清N' folding format byte-identical.

Style tag chips (STAGE_TAGS exactly: 极简/现代/玻璃拟态/暗色/暖色调/编辑风/科技感/大胆活力/柔和未来/瑞士国际), full-width .wz-ref cards true aspect, select ring + dark rbar, 🔍放大/🔗源/＋类似/✕/checkbox. Open Viewer (zoom/pan/keyboard) and confirm a background poll does NOT tear it down (refsViewerOpen guard). Refs grid must not flicker.

Dotted grid (radial #e3e3e1 1px @22px on #f7f7f6), default view {x:60,y:80,z:0.7}, zoom clamp 0.08-3, #cv-zoom % readout, controls +/- /fit, .cv-hint, #cv-empty. Pan/drag a card and verify: drag delta divided by zoom tracks cursor at any scale; wheel zoom anchors at cursor; ctrl/trackpad-pinch zooms; horizontal wheel pans. Drag mid-Agent-update must NOT yank (cvDragging guard). Hero base ring; page card aspect follows platform (PC landscape 1440/1080 vs phone 1080/2340), platform matrix on/off badges. EDGE: drag then close tab within 600ms -> currently layout LOST (no beforeunload flush).

Right-docked 320px panel only when stage 3; '本次参考' thumbs with 图N codes in selectedRefs order, include/exclude checkbox renumbers codes; 生成记录 entries (生成/改图 blue badge, ts, ref/result counts, thumbs, prompt); collapse state. CRITICAL: insertRefCode caret position + 图N->--image N ordering byte-identical to old.

Dark overlay both modes; draw a box -> normalized 0-1 toFixed(4) coords; min size 0.02 rejected; feedback mode prompts per-box text, redraw mode shared #pv-instr; numbered .pvn labels + .pvx delete; action button label flips by mode+box count. CRITICAL: the structured feedback text string '区域(左X% 上Y% 宽W% 高H%):text' and the 3 divergent redraw paths (stage3 no-box->gen-heroes baseImage; stage3 box & stage4 any->/api/redraw) byte-identical. Post-send confirm()->rerun stage6 bridge present.

image (.full contain), html (opens new tab, no modal), text/md/json (<pre> escaped), mindmap (.box.wide 94vw/88vh, lazy d3+markmap, grayscale palette, autoFit, initialExpandLevel:-1). openOverview cross-stage aggregation ('P{id} {name} · count' headers, ★基调 marker). Esc closes modal+preview.

Drawer slide-in 372px, Enter=send/Shift+Enter=newline, scroll-stick within 30px of bottom, .has-unread red dot logic. Choices bar warm #fffaf0 cards, 阶段N label, option chips + free-text reply, optimistic removal. Both: typing during a poll must not be interrupted.

New: name/slug (autoSlug + CJK random fallback, slugEdited stops auto-derive), platform picker (PC/H5/APP cards + device SVG + primary radio + draggable priority), localStorage draft restore toast. Delete: two-tier (soft remove vs hard-delete gated on exact-name typing, red btn enable). Verify draft persists across reload and clears on create.

#update-bar ink banner '🆕 有新版本 vX (当前 vY)' + 立即更新 on BOTH pages (global /api/ paths); #compat-banner red warn when phases!=7 listing the 7 stage names verbatim; #toast bottom-center pill 2.2s auto-hide, cleared on stage switch.

missed.item: Dead/unused CSS for an older grouped-lane canvas layout: .cv-lane-label/.cv-grouplabel/.cv-mapcap/.cv-divider, the entire .cv-page family (.cv-page/.cv-page .pthumb/.pinfo/.pn/.pmeta/.pv/.pdot/.cv-page.ph) and .cv-page-add

The rewrite must NOT port these as features (no lane labels, group dividers, or 150px .cv-page cards exist in the running UI). Flag-don't-assume, like the wz-log dead-code note.

missed.where: console.html: cvSave debounce 1708, flushCanvasSave 1718, only caller is selectStage 1089 (canvas->board)
missed.why: Inventories note flushCanvasSave-on-stage-leave but none note that closing the tab / browser navigation / hard reload within the 600ms debounce window silently LOSES the last drag (no window beforeunload handler exists). Parity vs improvement decision: the rewrite should at minimum match (data loss on close) or fix it with a beforeunload flush — must be a conscious choice, not accidental.

When an artifact file is mid-regen/deleted (redraw, removeRef, gen-version), the shows the browser broken-image glyph. Home cards have p.missing/p.error broken-state but per-artifact imgs do not. The React port should decide whether to add graceful fallbacks; old behavior = raw broken glyph.

missed.item: pf_state.py CLI is a co-equal WRITER of nearly every backing file (not just the server)
missed.where: knowledge graph nodes pf_state.py + ENDPOINTS notes; state.json/pages.json/choices.json/explore.json/agent-log-*.jsonl all written by spawned agents via pf_state
missed.why: Dataflow/Endpoints inventories mention it in passing but it deserves first-class emphasis for the WS design: the server does NOT mediate agent writes, so an mtime-watcher (or inotify) per-project is the ONLY change-detection signal for those files. A naive 'push on my own POST' WS design would miss every agent-originated update. This is the single most load-bearing fact for channel correctness.

missed.item: agent-log phases gen-heroes and collect-ref have backing log files but are NOT surfaced by the current poller dispatch
missed.where: server.py known phases include gen-heroes/collect-ref; console.html per-stage tick (2521) only calls pollExplore for P3 hero state (reads explore.json request slot + heroGenFailed), never pollAgentLog for gen-heroes/collect-ref
missed.why: Dataflow inventory proposes agent-log:* channels but implies symmetric usage. For parity the rewrite should know P3 hero progress is read from explore.json's request slot, NOT from agent-log-gen-heroes.jsonl. Wiring those logs would be a behavior ADDITION; flag before doing it.

missed.item: 'working' flag and home cover URL derivation are time/path-derived with no file event
missed.why: Dataflow notes time-derived working in passing but it's an easy-to-drop subtlety: an mtime-only WS watcher will NOT flip 'working' false after 120s of inactivity (no write occurs). Needs a periodic tick on the projects channel, else stale '进行中' badges persist.

missed.why: cv.notes is loaded, defaulted, and POSTed back on every canvas save but is NEVER rendered (no consumer) — another silent passthrough field the rewrite must preserve in the canvas.json payload (to avoid wiping agent/legacy data) even though it draws nothing. Inventories list cv.notes as state but don't flag it as render-less passthrough.

The d3/markmap version must match the vendored one or the palette fn breaks — pin versions if bundling via npm.

missed.why: Endpoints inventory mentions it but it's worth surfacing as a migration constraint: if the React build changes /api/version response shape, the 'is an instance already running on 7717' detection (used by start scripts) breaks. Keep {app:'productflow',version} verbatim.

risks.item: Infinite-canvas pan/drag/zoom controller: manual Pointer Events + setPointerCapture, 3px move threshold, closest()-bail allowlist (textarea/.cv-base/.cv-page-del/.cv-del/.cv-plat/.cv-controls/#cv-stagebar/#hero-dialog), drag delta /zoom, cursor-anchored wheel zoom x=cx-(cx-x)*k
risks.mitigation: Port the transform math VERBATIM (cvApply/cvZoomAt/cvZoomBy/cvFit incl. cvFit's y-40/+320 padding asymmetry). Attach wheel via ref+addEventListener('wheel',h,{passive:false}) NOT JSX onWheel (else browser zoom/scroll leaks; ctrl+wheel=browser zoom). Model drag session as a useRef; mutate card transform imperatively during drag, commit to state only on pointerup. Replicate the closest()-bail OR give every interactive sub-element stopPropagation on pointerDown. Pause store->DOM sync while an isInteracting ref is set (replaces renderCanvas cvDragging/cvEditing bail).

risks.item: Annotation coordinate mapping (#pv-stage): normalized 0-1 frac() via getBoundingClientRect, toFixed(4) wire contract, min 0.02 mis-tap filter, two modes sharing the stage, structured Agent-parsed feedback text + 3 divergent redraw routing paths by stage
risks.mitigation: Keep coords normalized + toFixed(4) (Agent remaps to source pixels). Use Pointer Events + setPointerCapture + touch-action:none. Preserve the exact feedback string format and the stage3-no-box->gen-heroes-baseImage vs stage3-box/stage4->/api/redraw branching byte-for-byte. Replace window.prompt per-box with inline input but keep the per-box text model.

risks.item: Render-on-poll focus guards become 'don't clobber drafts' under WS (renderStageExtra/renderChoices/pollBrief/pollResearch/pollStageRun + refsViewerOpen + chat scroll-stick)
risks.mitigation: Keep ALL editable/in-progress fields as LOCAL component state decoupled from channel data: description/clarify/answers, stylePrefs/selectedRefs, pStageRun[n].instr, researchLog.instr, hd textarea, choice reply inputs, pv boxes. Merge incoming pushes only into non-edited fields. Never remount a panel containing a focused field.

risks.item: Brief confirm-lag / generating-failed state machine derived from server request-slot presence (not explicit flags)
risks.mitigation: Port the reconciliation guards verbatim (keep local confirmed=true until server catches up; derive generating from request['gen-summary'] present, failed from slot vanished without ready). Skipping this yields 'confirm->question flashes back' and 'spinner never stops after failure' bugs.

risks.item: Viewer.js + markmap (d3) imperative vendor libs operating on DOM/SVG nodes
risks.mitigation: Viewer: instantiate in useEffect on a ref'd hidden gallery, destroy()+recreate when refs list changes, and SKIP list reconciliation while viewer is open (or React unmounts the node Viewer holds). Markmap: memoize ensureMarkmap promise, render empty <svg ref>, Markmap.create on open, cleanup on close, load order d3-first, PIN the vendored versions (palette fn depends on node.state.path internals).

risks.item: Per-stage canvas isolation + debounced save targeting the LEAVING stage; no beforeunload flush
risks.mitigation: Capture target stage id at debounce-schedule time (closure, not current state) so an in-flight save hits the right stage cell. useEffect cleanup on stage change must flush. Add a beforeunload/pagehide flush (the old code lacks one -> drag-then-close loses layout); decide parity vs fix explicitly. Preserve _CANVAS_LOCK semantics + per-stage cell merge; do NOT echo own canvas writes back over WS.

risks.item: agent-log:stage running/waiting come from in-memory _STAGE_RUNNING (no file) + choices.json; 409 concurrency
risks.mitigation: WS server must push stage status on _STAGE_RUNNING mutation (run-stage/run-action add; _auto_stage/_auto_action finally discard) AND on choices.json change (waiting recompute via _has_open_choice). Carry data.running/data.waiting in the push, not just the jsonl tail. Preserve double-click guard (pStageRun.running) + surface HTTP 409 already_running as toast. run-stage and run-action share the (pid,6) guard and stage-6 channel.

risks.item: localStorage new-project draft (pf-new-project-draft) + native HTML5 drag-reorder priority + autoSlug CJK fallback
risks.mitigation: useEffect syncing nm->localStorage on every change, load-on-open with restore toast, clear only on successful create. Keep splice-reorder semantics for priority DnD, primary auto-reselect when its platform is deselected, slugEdited flag, and the CJK->random fb slug fallback.

risks.item: WS handshake on a stdlib ThreadingHTTPServer (BaseHTTPRequestHandler has no Upgrade support) + repointing static serving to dist/
risks.mitigation: Either hand-roll the Sec-WebSocket-Accept upgrade inside do_GET and hijack self.connection on its own thread (keeps zero-dependency design), or front with a minimal WS-capable server. Apply the SAME _host_ok (127.0.0.1 only) + _post_origin_ok (Origin/Sec-Fetch-Site) checks to the WS upgrade and validate Origin. Repoint _console() to dist/index.html (keep no-store), add a hashed-asset static handler (mirror /vendor content-type dict, long-cache hashed names), keep /p/<pid>/artifacts/ as a SEPARATE base, keep VERSION {app:'productflow'} shape, preserve ID_RE/CRED_KEY_RE + path-traversal + project-delete safety gates + creds masking.

risks.item: FLICKER on every push: 6 sites rebuild (project covers, board #gallery cache-busted ?t=, P2 refs, P3 hero+dialog thumbs, P4 page thumbs, whole #cv-world)
risks.mitigation: Make all keyed React components (key by id + version/file), keep img src stable; only change artUrl ?t=ts when the file actually changed so React diffs instead of dropping/recreating nodes. Never rebuild #cv-world wholesale; transform on a stable container.

critic.summary: The four inventories are accurate and unusually complete on the documented API surface, state stores, render sites, and interaction libs — the ENDPOINTS inventory also correctly corrects the prompt's route shape (everything project-scoped is under /p//...). Cross-checking against the real console.html (2538 lines) and server.py (1866 lines) plus the knowledge graph, the gaps are all in edge-cases and silent-passthrough/dead code rather than missing features: (1) a whole family of canvas CSS classes (.cv-lane-label/.cv-grouplabel/.cv-mapcap/.cv-divider/.cv-page*/.cv-page-add) is defined but NEVER emitted in JS — dead leftover of an older grouped-lane layout; do not port as features. (2) There is no beforeunload flush, so a drag within the 600ms canvas-save debounce is lost on tab close. (3) There is zero fallback — broken/mid-regen artifacts show the browser broken glyph. (4) A separate navigator.platform OS sniff (Mac vs other) drives the 📂 reveal-code button label, distinct from the wizard.primary platform copy. (5) cv.notes is loaded/POSTed but never rendered — a render-less passthrough that must be preserved. (6) The 'working' home badge and the gen-heroes/collect-ref agent-logs are time/path-derived or unsurfaced, so an mtime-only watcher will mis-handle them. The single most load-bearing channel fact: pf_state.py (the agent CLI) is a co-equal writer of state/pages/choices/explore/agent-log files that the server never mediates, so per-project mtime/inotify watching — not 'push on my own POST' — is the only correct WS trigger. I proposed 13 push channels (home/global: projects, system/version; per-project: state, inbox, health, pages, choices, brief, explore, agent-log:research/search-refs/stage-, wizard) with canvas-layout and deploy-creds kept as client-owned request/response, all mutations staying POST with results flowing back via the matching channel; the highest-risk ports remain the manual pan/drag/zoom math, normalized annotation coordinates, the focus-guard-to-WS reconciliation, the in-memory _STAGE_RUNNING running/waiting flags, and the WS upgrade on the stdlib ThreadingHTTPServer.
